function_summary: Demand planning, supply planning, procurement, fulfillment center operations, transportation, ops integration. Manages factory physics of getting product from vendor to member doorstep across multiple FCs.
key_workflows: End-of-shift handoffs across three FCs; carrier scorecarding and exception monitoring; PO/ETA tracking; vendor QBR prep; inbound transportation tendering; packaging compliance tracking; delivered-quality root-cause deep dives.
pain_points: Shift handoffs assembled manually from 6+ spreadsheets; carrier scorecards stitched from 4+ data sources; outlier review of SKU forecasts is manual; last-mile cost data scattered across vendors; defect root-causing only covers top-of-list exceptions.
current_tools: Snowflake Cortex, Parabola, Claude Code for internal tools (Mission Control, Capacinator, Handoff Generator), Gemini for site-level analysis.
exercise_1_theme: Pull end-of-shift metrics, safety incidents, open issues, and staffing gaps from multiple structured sources into a single handoff brief ranked by what the incoming shift lead needs to know first.
exercise_2_theme: Compare on-time, exception rate, and cost across multiple carriers; flag underperformers and surface negotiation talking points by lane.

function_summary: Brand/Mission/Creative; Member Engagement (CRM, lifecycle, analytics); Member Growth (acquisition, paid creative, BD/influencer); Member Services floor (chat/voice, QA, WFM). Combined function spanning member acquisition through ongoing engagement and support resolution.
key_workflows: Creative variant testing on paid channels; CRM lifecycle deep dives; weekly revenue and KPI readouts; community management across social platforms; Voice-of-Member ticket synthesis; QA review of support agent conversations; new-hire training for floor agents.
pain_points: Creative variant testing capped by manual review; ad fatigue caught after performance decays; community management consumes 40+ hrs/week; VOM insight extraction requires manual ticket combing; QA team can only review a small fraction of agent conversations.
current_tools: Claude Cowork, Amplitude AI, Cortex AI, Adobe Firefly + Premiere, Midjourney, Sierra (chat/voice automation), Zendesk QA, Gemini agent copilot, Bynder DAM, Motion App, Foreplay.
exercise_1_theme: Synthesize a week of support tickets, social mentions, and survey verbatims into a 'what members are telling us' brief with emerging themes ranked by signal strength.
exercise_2_theme: Read weekly performance across multiple creative variants; flag which to scale, which to retire, and what new variants to test next.

function_summary: Category Management, Owned Brand, Merch Marketing, Food Safety/Quality (FSQA), Retail Operations. Owns product selection, vendor relationships, pricing, ingredient compliance, promotional placement, and the catalog itself.
key_workflows: Ingredient compliance review for new products; vendor JBP/scorecard preparation; selection-gap monitoring vs competitors; FC quality inspection management; pricing analysis across regions and retailers; promotional brief generation for cross-category campaigns; FSQA testing standards maintenance.
pain_points: Ingredient vetting takes 45min to several hours per complex SKU; JBP decks pulled together manually from multiple sources for each negotiation; pricing monitored across 7K+ SKUs in 10 regions is too much for the pricing team; selection monitoring is fully manual.
current_tools: Snowflake Business Intelligence + Cortex, Claude Cowork, Gemini for tariff validation, Lovable + Cursor for internal portals, Syndigo (PIM), Novi (ingredient platform), Clear Demand (pricing).
exercise_1_theme: Take a raw product ingredient list and a quality-standards document; output a flagged per-ingredient breakdown (acceptable / restricted / unacceptable / docs-required) with the rationale and next-step action for each line.
exercise_2_theme: Compile a vendor's sales trend, in-stock rate, lead time, growth vs prior year, and recent quality issues into a one-page negotiation prep with concrete talking points and asks.

function_summary: FP&A (month-end close, AOP variance, executive flash, FBP support to business leaders); Accounting (general ledger, accruals, AP, vendor management, SOX controls). Lean team supporting all functions with monthly close, variance analysis, and ad-hoc business questions.
key_workflows: Month-end close (~50% of time spent on data prep before analysis); variance commentary against AOP and prior year; Executive Flash and CFO deck production; AP bill-variance research against POs and receipts; vendor credit request submission and dispute tracking; ad-hoc FBP analysis across business units.
pain_points: Close lag: Executive Flash takes 2+ days after lock; AP variance research coordinated manually across vendors, FCs, category managers; merchandise invoices with discrepancies investigated one at a time; FBP analytical depth limited by dependency on data team for routine queries.
current_tools: Parabola (workflow automation for accounting), Claude Cowork (modeling and analysis), Snowflake Cortex (self-serve SQL), Coupa (AP), Hiver (vendor email).
exercise_1_theme: Compare current-month actuals to budget and prior year by line item; rank the top variances by materiality and generate first-pass commentary tied to known business drivers.
exercise_2_theme: Reconcile a batch of merchandise invoices against PO and receipt data; flag variances above tolerance with suggested resolution path (price discrepancy, quantity short, missing receipt, vendor billing error).

function_summary: Talent Acquisition (HQ + FC/Last Mile recruiting); HQ People Operations and HRBPs; FC People Operations; Legal/Compliance (contracts, regulatory, marketing review, trademark). Combined function spanning hiring through employee lifecycle, plus legal coverage for the whole business.
key_workflows: Inbound resume review at high volume; interview coordination; performance management cycles; employee inquiry handling (policies, benefits, leaves); ER documentation and follow-ups; contract review and redlining (SaaS, NDA, vendor, influencer); regulatory research and monitoring; marketing/UX content review against compliance.
pain_points: Recruiter and coordinator time consumed by manual screening and scheduling before any value-add starts; performance review admin (50%+ reduction recently with Lattice but more possible); contract review cycles measured in weeks for some categories; marketing content only reviewed at a fraction of volume produced.
current_tools: Covey (resume parsing), Fountain (FC hiring), Lattice (performance), Claude (drafting/research), CoCounsel (legal research), DocuSign IAM/Agreement Desk (contract triage), Relyance AI (privacy), Synthesia/Beautiful.ai (training content), Gemini (pulse survey, comms).
exercise_1_theme: Paste a vendor contract and a company contract playbook; output a flagged section-by-section review (in-policy / soft deviation / hard deviation), suggested redlines, and a one-paragraph risk summary for the requester.
exercise_2_theme: Pull together engagement survey verbatims, recent exit interview themes, and HR ticket trends into a department-by-department brief showing what's actually going on under the headline scores.
